mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive mean when FEDFUNDS exceeds 4.0%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals an imminent labor market contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within the next trading week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE increases by more than 0.2 percentage points over three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint exceedance of thresholds for RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns with a statistical significance of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production during low-inflation periods signals sustainable economic growth without triggering aggressive central bank tightening, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: The positive predictive power of INDPRO growth vanishes or reverses when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%, or Rank IC drops below 0.01 in high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: The coexistence of elevated consumer price inflation and restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses real discount rates and corporate margins, forcing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of observations where both CPIAUCSL exceeds 315 and FEDFUNDS remains above 4.0% simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates a resilient labor market that sustains consumer spending and corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.02, or 60-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with payroll additions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate exceeding 1.5 percent confirms robust aggregate demand that supports equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Instances where GDPC1 qoq growth surpasses 1.5% yield negative 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases over a 5-year walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent contraction in industrial output and deterioration in employment conditions confirms a broad economic slowdown, triggering immediate risk-off flows and negative S&P 500 returns within the next trading week.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines 2 months AND UNRATE rises
failure_condition: The joint signal of declining INDPRO and rising UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a hit rate above 50% in out-of-sample testing, or Rank IC remains below 0.03.
